text.text:
Came for the hype and was not disappointed. Enjoy my banana cream Matcha. It was not too sweet and had the perfect balance.

Got the regular soft bread as well as the salt bread with the red bean filling. I preferred the latter as the plain salt bread was a little too plain. It is also more of a Japanese milk bread soft texture rather than a flaky texture.

We got a few more pastries to try as well. I really liked the takoyaki bread and the sausage bun. The Matcha mochi bun as well as the honey toast with red bean filling was just OK. Overall, really interesting selection of pastries. Will return again.

We got a few more pastries to try as well. I really liked the takoyaki bread and the sausage bun. The Matcha mochi bun as well as the honey toast with red bean filling was just OK. Overall, really interesting selection of pastries. Will return again.

text.text: The crispiest top layer but softest salt bread I’ve ever eaten! Such a cute and soft lighted interior! I’m no baking expert but the flour they use is something special! I have to go back and try all their other goods!!

originalText.text: The crispiest top layer but softest salt bread I’ve ever eaten! Such a cute and soft lighted interior! I’m no baking expert but the flour they use is something special! I have to go back and try all their other goods!!

text.text:
Would have given 5 stars without the 40 minutes wait LOL. And I am talking about the line being up to Blick Art only’, as I have seen some people seeing line up to the street corner.

Their servers delivered top-notch service though. Warm welcome and very helpful even they didn’t seem fluent enough in English, which was so appreciated.

Decent despite not many sit-down spaces to dine indoor. We got their Takoyaki, Curry Pan, Japanini, and Anpan/red bean salt bread. The takoyaki was literally the octopus ball in soft fluffy bread with the sauce itself being so flavorful. The curry pan had generous filling and rich seasoning; bit greasy for my liking but expected as it’s indeed fried one. Love the Japanini with chunks of bacon and abundant melted cheese; so tasty especially when served warm. My top pick though was the sweet one - red bean. Their salt bread being so moist with nice milky touch was certainly the key, but the twist of sweet red bean paste with salty bread top created stellar duo.

text.text:
I stopped by this café and had the matcha latte, which was really good — smooth, nicely balanced, and made with their own house oat milk, which gave it a unique and creamy flavor. It felt like a thoughtful touch that made the drink stand out a bit more.

I got there around 1:30 pm, but unfortunately all the pastries were already sold out, which was a bit disappointing since I was hoping to try some. It seems like a popular spot, and I just wish they stayed open a bit longer or had more pastries available later in the day.

Still, the matcha alone made it worth the stop, and I’d definitely come back earlier next time to catch the full selection.

Still, the matcha alone made it worth the stop, and I’d definitely come back earlier next time to catch the full selection.

text.text:
I came here Mon 8:08am and there were only a few people in front of me. Around 8:30am, it got bustling and no available seats. Come early if you can to grab some of these pastries!

Pastry 3.5/5.
My fav was salt and butter. Smooth, soft, yet crispy at the bottom. I love red bean so def the filling one was great. Curry was my next fav. Something savory, good flavor. Wish it had more chunkiness of food. Melon was not my fav. I recently came back from japan and their convenient store melon pan was better. The sugary part on top is too hard. As i bite down it cracks. Wish it was more crusty hard, not hardened sugar texture. Inside the bread was not fluffy white bread but hard packed dry white bread. Matcha melon was better but still it was disappointing compared to the japanese convenient store melon pan i had (i traveled to osaka and kyoto, so maybe different style at hokkaido??). Overall I really wanted to love these pastries, but not worth $4.45ish~ per item price.

Original Matcha Latte iced 3/5.
I got it half sweetened. The color looks great, but it tastes blend and watery. My fav matcha is rok in k-town because it reminds me of the ice matcha i had in kyoto. Strong matcha, slightly sweet, creamy yet easy to drink. This one seems to be basic. I can make it at home with nice expensive matcha powder.
